notes:
đánh = to hit or strike. That’s the core meaning: one thing makes forceful contact with another.

It’s a super flexible verb: you can đánh + person/animal/object (physical hit), but also for activities like playing instruments or sports, typing, brushing teeth, applying makeup, or marking something. Context tells you which meaning.

Very common extended meanings: “to play” (an instrument/game), “to type,” “to brush,” “to mark.” Think of it as “apply force/action to something.”
exampleSentencesTranslated: You should not hit your younger brother when angry.
She is learning how to hit the drum in the school band.
The children like to go outside to hit the ball together.

definitions.en: to hit, strike, or play (an instrument)

example.en: She plays the drums very well.
example.es: Ella toca la batería muy bien.
example.vi: Cô ấy đánh trống rất hay.

exampleSentences: Anh không nên đánh em trai khi tức giận.
Chị ấy học cách đánh trống trong ban nhạc của trường.
Bọn trẻ thích ra sân chơi để đánh bóng cùng nhau.

Very common extended meanings: “to play” (an instrument/game), “to type,” “to brush,” “to mark.” Think of it as “apply force/action to something.”
